FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class D3D12Device

Namespace
FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12
Assembly
FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.dll

The reference Windows RHI backend (design/subsystems/pal-rhi.md, gpu-renderer.md). Real D3D12: hardware device, DIRECT command queue + fence, a DXGI flip-model swapchain on the HWND, an RTV heap, and per-frame record→submit→present. Step 1 clears; the SDF rounded-rect pipeline and the DirectWrite glyph atlas layer on top.

public sealed class D3D12Device : IGpuDevice, IDisposable

BackendName

Property Value

string

BackendNameSuffix 

public string BackendNameSuffix { get; }

Property Value

string

DiagGpuDetail

One-line GPU residency summary (glyph atlas + image texture store) for the MemCensus GpuDetail hook. Reads the stores' census accessors; null until the device is initialized. Tiny fixed-bucket sums (never per-frame).

public string DiagGpuDetail { get; }

Property Value

string

DiagResourceTotals

Tracked live D3D12 resource totals (bytes + count) from FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.D3D12MemoryDiagnostics — an O(1) read of the running tally for the MemCensus sampler.

public (long bytes, int count) DiagResourceTotals { get; }

Property Value

(long bytes, int count)

SizePx 

public Size2 SizePx { get; }

Property Value

Size2

Methods

CaptureBgra(out int, out int)

Debug-only: read the last-rendered back buffer back to CPU as tightly-packed, top-down BGRA8. Used by the --screenshot tooling to produce a PNG for visual fidelity diffing — NOT a hot-path method (it stalls the GPU). With FLIP_DISCARD the just-presented buffer at _frameIndex is still intact until reused.

public byte[] CaptureBgra(out int width, out int height)

Parameters

width int
height int

Returns

byte[]

CreateSwapchain(in SwapchainDesc) 

public ISwapchain CreateSwapchain(in SwapchainDesc desc)

Parameters

desc SwapchainDesc

Returns

ISwapchain

Dispose()

Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources.

public void Dispose()

EvictImage(int)

The residency manager evicted imageId — release its GPU texture (deferred behind the frame fence so an in-flight frame can't read freed memory). No-op if not resident.

public void EvictImage(int imageId)

Parameters

imageId int

SubmitDrawList(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<ulong>, in FrameInfo)

Record + batch + submit the per-frame DrawList. drawList is the POD command stream.

public void SubmitDrawList(ReadOnlySpan<byte> drawList, ReadOnlySpan<ulong> sortKeys, in FrameInfo ctx)

Parameters

drawList ReadOnlySpan<byte>
sortKeys ReadOnlySpan<ulong>
ctx FrameInfo

UploadImage(int, ReadOnlySpan<byte>, int, int)

Hand decoded PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 pixels for imageId to the backend (the media-pipeline §4.1 texture upload). The backend create-or-replaces a resident texture (or atlas page) keyed by id and samples it from the DrawImage opcode. pbgra8 is valid only for this call — the backend copies it into its texture-staging ring; it is never retained. Rows may not be 256-aligned; the backend pads. Called once per decode completion, before SubmitDrawList(ReadOnlySpan<byte>, ReadOnlySpan<ulong>, in FrameInfo).

public void UploadImage(int imageId, ReadOnlySpan<byte> pbgra8, int w, int h)

Parameters

imageId int
pbgra8 ReadOnlySpan<byte>
w int
h int
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
